title: Lastrea brachypoda
taxonomy: Lastrea brachypoda is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns that were previously classified under the genus Nephrolepis. The family Dryopteridaceae consists of about 1,200 species of ferns, commonly known as wood ferns or shield ferns. Lastrea brachypoda is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The species is often found growing on rocks, trees, and other substrates in its native habitats.
uses: Lastrea brachypoda is traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In folk medicine, the plant is applied topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Its antioxidant properties are also believed to help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Additionally, Lastrea brachypoda is thought to have immunomodulatory effects, boosting the body's immune system to fight off infections. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Lastrea brachypoda for medicinal purposes.
